 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","title":"[PACK] secrets.vault - HashiCorp Vault Pack","description":"# HashiCorp Vault Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for HashiCorp Vault CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `vault secrets disable` - Disables secrets engine (data loss)
- `vault kv delete` - Deletes secrets
- `vault kv destroy` - Permanently destroys secret versions
- `vault kv metadata delete` - Deletes all versions
- `vault auth disable` - Disables auth method
- `vault policy delete` - Removes policies
- `vault token revoke` - Revokes tokens
- `vault lease revoke` - Revokes leases

## Safe Patterns
- `vault read`, `vault list`
- `vault kv get`, `vault kv list`
- `vault status`, `vault version`
- `vault audit list`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: vault
- Consider namespace operations for enterprise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All destructive patterns blocked
- [ ] Safe patterns not blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Vault workflows
- [ ] Documentation includes examples","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:00.911143116-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: secrets.vault pack already present in repo (src/packs/secrets/vault.rs + tests) and registered in pack registry. Docs update delegated to mbq7.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.083179678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.124770231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.167848674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.208877791-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.24822327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.288944838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.129072418-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.172995575-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","title":"Implement ast-grep-core pattern matching layer","description":"# ast-grep-core Pattern Matching Layer

## Objective

Integrate ast-grep-core as a Rust library for structural pattern matching in heredoc/inline-script content.

## Architecture decision

Prefer **embedded ast-grep-core** over shelling out to a CLI, because:

- avoids per-invocation process spawn overhead
- keeps a single-binary distribution story
- enables richer match metadata (spans) for `dcg explain`

(Still subject to ADR confirmation; this task implements whichever embedded approach is chosen.)

## Dependencies to add (tentative)

```toml
[dependencies]
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = [
    "bash",
    "python",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "ruby"
    # Note: Perl may not be available
] }
```

## API design

```rust
/// Pattern matcher using ast-grep-core
pub struct AstMatcher {
    patterns: HashMap<Language, Vec<CompiledPattern>>,
}

impl AstMatcher {
    /// Create matcher with default destructive patterns
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Load patterns from config / registry
    pub fn from_config(config: &PatternConfig) -> Result<Self>;

    /// Match patterns against code (bounded)
    pub fn find_matches(&self, code: &str, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<PatternMatch>, MatchError>;
}

/// Result of pattern matching
pub struct PatternMatch {
    pub rule_id: String,             // stable: pack_id:pattern_name
    pub reason: String,
    pub matched_text_preview: String,
    pub start: usize,                // byte offset (or row/col)
    pub end: usize,
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub severity: Severity,          // ties into decision policy
}

pub enum Severity { Error, Warning, Info }
```

## Pattern definition format (example)

Patterns should be defined and reviewed under `git_safety_guard-6sg` (pattern library structure). The matching layer must be agnostic to *what* the patterns are, but must support:

- stable IDs
- per-pattern severity
- per-pattern suggestions (optional)

## Error handling / fail-open

In hook mode:

- Unsupported language → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Parse error → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Timeout → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker

In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`:

- return structured diagnostics (not silent skip)

## Performance targets (initial)

- Pattern compilation: one-time at startup (bounded)
- Parse: <2ms for typical heredoc sizes
- Match: <1ms typical
- Total AST path: bounded by a hard timeout

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements an embedded AST matcher that can:
  - parse at least one supported language
  - match at least one simple pattern
  - return match spans + line number
- Enforces a hard timeout for AST work and fails open in hook mode on timeout.
- Produces match metadata compatible with:
  - `dcg explain` highlighting (matched span + preview)
  - rule attribution (stable rule_id)
  - decision policy (severity)
- Includes unit tests for:
  - a positive match
  - a negative (safe) snippet
  - an unsupported-language path
  - a timeout path (if testable)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive control
This layer must support the FP-immunity strategy, not undermine it:

- matchers should attach stable rule IDs + severity so policy can default-warn vs deny
- matchers should support negative fixtures / “must-not-match” tests per rule family
- prefer composite/high-signal rules (AST match + literal destructive payload) over broad matches (e.g., `exec($VAR)`)

### Tests
- For each supported language: at least one positive and one negative snippet.
- Span/line mapping test: matches return accurate location for explain output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:24.313325332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ast-grep-core pattern matching layer with AstMatcher struct, find_matches(), timeout handling, and 17 unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.909572665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-du4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.929896409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:18.965756128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv","title":"Self-Update Mechanism","description":"# Epic: Self-Update Mechanism

## Priority Score: 680/1000 (High Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Users should be able to update DCG with a single command. The self_update crate provides this capability for Rust CLIs, integrating with GitHub Releases for seamless updates.

A built-in update mechanism:
- Reduces friction for staying current
- Ensures security fixes reach users quickly
- Provides version checking without manual effort
- Builds user confidence in the tool

## Research Findings (2025-2026)

### self_update crate (v0.41+)

The self_update crate provides:
- GitHub Releases integration
- Platform-specific binary detection
- In-place binary replacement
- Version comparison (semver)
- Progress reporting during download
- Checksum verification support

### Update Flow

1. Check current version against latest GitHub release
2. If newer version available, prompt user
3. Download platform-specific binary
4. Verify checksum
5. Replace current binary atomically
6. Report success

## Example Usage

Check for updates:
dcg update --check
> Current version: 1.9.0
> Latest version:  2.0.0
> Run 'dcg update' to upgrade

Perform update:
dcg update
> Downloading dcg v2.0.0...
> Verifying checksum...
> Replacing binary...
> Successfully updated to v2.0.0!

## Technical Approach

### 1. Add self_update dependency

Cargo.toml:
self_update = { version = "0.41", features = ["compression-zip-deflate"] }

### 2. Implement update command

New src/update.rs module with:
- check_for_update() - Returns Option<ReleaseInfo>
- perform_update() - Downloads and replaces binary
- Version comparison using semver

### 3. CLI Integration

dcg update [--check] [--force] [--version <version>]

--check: Only check, dont update
--force: Update even if already current
--version: Update to specific version (for rollback)

## Key Files

- Cargo.toml - Add self_update dependency
- src/update.rs - New: Update logic
- src/cli.rs - Add update subcommand
- src/main.rs - Wire up update command

## Version Notification

Optionally show update notice on regular commands:
- Check for updates in background (cached for 24h)
- If update available, show subtle notice
- Respect --quiet flag

## Rollback Support

Allow updating to specific older version:
dcg update --version 1.8.0

This provides escape hatch if new version has issues.

## Security Considerations

- Always verify checksums before replacement
- Use HTTPS for all downloads
- Atomic file replacement (write temp, then rename)
- Preserve file permissions
- Clean up on failure

## Success Criteria

- dcg update --check shows version info
- dcg update successfully updates binary
- Checksum verification mandatory
- Rollback to previous versions works
- Update notice shown optionally
- Works on all supported platforms

## Dependencies

Requires GitHub release checksum generation from Auto-Install epic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:51.034606009-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:55:51.034606009-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-b6vb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:53.220234834-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.2","title":"[Update] Implement binary update mechanism","description":"Implement the actual binary update mechanism.

## Implementation

In src/update.rs:
- perform_update() - Download and replace binary
- Backup current binary before replacement
- Atomic file replacement (download to temp, then rename)
- Platform-specific binary selection

CLI command: dcg update [--force]

## Update Flow

1. Check if update available
2. Prompt for confirmation (unless --force)
3. Download platform-specific binary
4. Verify checksum
5. Backup current binary
6. Atomic replace
7. Report success

## Error Handling

- Network failures should not corrupt installation
- Partial downloads should be cleaned up
- Clear error messages for each failure mode

## Acceptance Criteria

- Binary updates successfully
- Backup created before update
- Atomic replacement prevents corruption
- --force flag skips confirmation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:44.990802124-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:44.990802124-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:44.992392539-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:52.986691641-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.3","title":"[Update] Add background update notification","description":"Add background update notification shown on regular commands.

## Implementation

Using update-informer crate pattern:
- Spawn background thread to check for updates
- Cache result for 24 hours
- Show subtle notice if update available

## Example Output

After running any dcg command:
! A new version of dcg is available: 1.9.0 -> 2.0.0
  Run 'dcg update' to upgrade

## Configuration

Respect these settings:
- --quiet flag suppresses notice
- DCG_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1 env var disables check
- Config option: check_updates = false

## Acceptance Criteria

- Notice shown only when update available
- Does not slow down normal operation (background check)
- Respects quiet/no-check settings
- Cache prevents repeated API calls","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.238895992-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.238895992-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.240706732-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:53.049737914-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.4","title":"[Update] Implement version rollback","description":"Implement version rollback capability.

## Implementation

- Keep up to 3 previous binary backups
- Allow rollback to specific version
- List available versions command

CLI commands:
dcg update --rollback         # Rollback to previous
dcg update --rollback v1.8.0  # Rollback to specific version
dcg update --list-versions    # Show available backups

## Backup Storage

Location: ~/.local/share/dcg/backups/
Format: dcg-<version>-<timestamp>

## Acceptance Criteria

- Rollback restores previous binary
- Specific version rollback works
- Old backups pruned (keep max 3)
- Clear listing of available versions","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.493847736-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.493847736-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:45.495135271-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f9fv.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:18:53.108117699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
